calculated_metrics.confidence_note: This unit cost is estimated by PRISM by dividing total programme expenditure by the total quantified animal beneficiaries.

details.calculation: ($163,356 USD / 8,422 total beneficiaries) = $19.40 USD per outcome. | A $1,000 USD donation achieves ≈ 52 outcomes.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Unspecified: 100%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.

Expenses against Grants

Donation and Grants

TOTAL
This year, Kalimpong Animal Shelter treated 5054 animals, while Darjeeling Animal Shelter, operating as its sister unit, treated over 3368 animals, giving them renewed chances at life.
For 26 years, the Darjeeling Goodwill Animal Shelter has been committed to animal welfare, making a profound impact through programmes such as Animal Birth Control (ABC) and Anti-rabies Vaccinations.

metrics.context_qualifier: Total animals receiving clinical treatment at Kalimpong and Darjeeling Animal Shelters.

source.quote: Total - 8422 Clinical Treatment of Animals April 2023 to March 2024 (In 12 Months)

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs and cats vaccinated against rabies in Kalimpong and Darjeeling.

source.quote: Anti-Rabies Vaccination (Dogs & Cats) Total = 4310 (In 12 months) April 2023 To March 2024

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs and cats sterilized through the ABC program in Kalimpong and Darjeeling.

source.quote: Total 1259 Dogs & 316 Cats (ABC) April 2023 to March 2024 (In 12 Months)

metrics.context_qualifier: Dogs rescued by Kalimpong and Darjeeling Animal Shelters.

source.quote: In 2023, the sister concerns of DGAS - Kalimpong Animal Shelter & Darjeeling Animal Shelter rescued a total of 612 dogs.

metrics.context_qualifier: Puppies and kittens successfully adopted into forever homes.

source.quote: This year, DGAS successfully found loving forever homes to 160 puppies and 33 kittens, giving them a second chance at life.

metrics.context_qualifier: Percentage of sick animals supported by the shelters making a strong recovery.

source.quote: Despite outbreaks of viral diseases, such as canine parvovirus and feline illnesses, the shelters managed to support 90% of the sick animals in making a strong recovery.

Since 1998, the Kalimpong Animal Shelter has been instrumental in managing the stray dog population in Kalimpong and controlling rabies through its Animal Birth Control (ABC) and Anti-Rabies Vaccination Programmes.

significant_events.summary: Ongoing program for managing stray dog population and controlling rabies through catch, spay/neuter, and release.

significant_events.event_name: Anti-Rabies Vaccination Camp / DAS Rabies Project

source.quote: This year, the Darjeeling Animal Shelter successfully vaccinated 2648 dogs against rabies across all 32 wards, marking a significant achievement in its ongoing efforts to create a safe environment for all.

significant_events.summary: Extensive vaccination drives across Darjeeling wards, focusing on stray and community dogs, with awareness and education.

significant_events.event_name: Animal Relief efforts in the wake of Teesta flash flood

source.quote: In October, with the support of Help Animals India, the shelter provided urgent disaster relief in response to the devastating flash floods in Teesta, Kalimpong, Rangpo, Bangey, and Najok.

significant_events.summary: Provided urgent aid, medical care, food, rehabilitation, and vaccinations to animals affected by devastating flash floods.

source.quote: Darjeeling Animal Shelter further broadened its impact by organising multiple outreach programmes, educating children and community stakeholders on rabies prevention and fostering compassion for animals.

significant_events.summary: Engages children and community stakeholders on rabies prevention, animal welfare, and compassion through outreach and shelter tours.

source.quote: For the first time, Darjeeling now has its own SPCA, dedicated to tackling these critical issues and advancing animal welfare throughout the region.

significant_events.summary: First SPCA in Darjeeling established with support from Humane Society International (HSI) India to address animal cruelty and advance welfare.

source.quote: Over the past three years, the Darjeeling Animal Shelter (DAS) has been fortunate to receive substantial support for its Animal Birth Control (ABC) Programme from Dogs Trust Worldwide (DTW).

significant_events.summary: Partnership providing substantial support for ABC and ARV programs, expanding efforts in sterilisation and mass vaccination.

significant_events.event_name: Collaboration with Marchig Animal Welfare Trust

source.quote: Kalimpong Animal Shelter, supported by the Marchig Animal Welfare Trust, launched a spay and neuter campaign this year, aimed at helping felines lead healthy and stress-free lives.

significant_events.summary: Support for a spay and neuter campaign for cats in Kalimpong to address growing feline population and cruelty.

significant_events.event_name: First- Aid Training Programme for Small Animals

source.quote: On August 23, 2023, the Darjeeling Animal Shelter (DAS) and Humane Society International (HSI) jointly organised a first aid training session for small animals at the Animal Resources Department GTA in Darjeeling, drawing over 50 animal enthusiasts and advocates from Darjeeling and neighbouring towns.

significant_events.summary: Training session for over 50 animal enthusiasts and advocates on providing on-the-spot treatment and first aid for injured animals.

source.quote: The dedicated KAS team, including Vet Dr. Deo and team members Lil, Lakpa, Sanjeev, and Roshan, worked tirelessly until late in the evening, successfully sterilising a total of 37 dogs and 11 cats.

significant_events.summary: Free ABC camp in Kalimpong, sterilizing 37 dogs and 11 cats, with 48 animals receiving care.

source.quote: All dogs are released so that they can thrive in their own free environment, as dogs should not live in confinement; their lives are better in their own spaces.
Occasionally, the treatment rooms reach full capacity, leading to a surge in cases that require attention, but our dedicated team continues to serve, cure, care and comfort.

data.original_text: Founded by Dr. Christine Townend in the year 1992 for the sole purpose of Animal Birth Control of street dogs that were suffering with genital problems , skin conditions and overburdened with a litter of puppies that were neither adopted nor fostered.
The female dogs and litter of puppies had to succumb a life of despair and cruelty because the locals feared of rabies and female dogs procreating time and again.
About 22 years ago, rabies, a fatal disease/virus was on the rise in India and in these two Indian districts under West Bengal, Kalimpong and Darjeeling.

The Municipality unanimously agreed to this approach then a new hope of positivity dawned on Dr. Christine by starting an animal shelter with a dispensary set-up for the near future well-being of street dogs where animals could be treated, rescued, caught for sterilisation and sheltered post-operative care for a few days and then be released.
With this, Kalimpong Animal Shelter enacted the ABC Programme in 1998 and a few years later, the same Programme was extended to Darjeeling Goodwill Animal Shelter, a sister shelter.
Dog creche providing healthcare, sterilization, and vaccination services to improve the lives of street dogs.
The shelter started an Animal Birth Control (ABC) program, focusing on spaying and vaccinating dogs.
The staff faced challenges in identifying stray dogs from owned ones, leading to an education campaign to locate dog owners and promote the benefits of spaying.
Over the years so many dogs have been spayed and vaccinated that, not only has the health of the community dog population greatly increased, but the population of puppies has been greatly reduced.
These days there are so few puppies available in Kalimpong town, that Darjeeling Animal Shelter sends surplus pups from Darjeeling to KAS for adoption.
In addition to the ABC programme, the staff at KAS conducted many camps in far-lying villages only accessible by walking.
KAS has saved the lives of thousands of animals since its foundation in 1993.
Due to the work of DAS and KAS, together with the Darjeeling and Kalimpong Municipalities, all poisoning of street dogs has stopped from 1998 onwards.
The street dogs are now safe and friendly.
Puppies are not being born to die an early death from starvation and disease.

The Chairman of both Darjeeling and Kalimpong Municipalities agreed to stop poisoning all dogs and since then KAS and DAS have successfully carried out ABC programmes. No further dogs in either Municipality have been poisoned.
